ranks.name: Unranked
ranks.description: Not formally recognized by the Guild.
ranks.evaluation_hint: Starting state for new players or visitors.

ranks.name: E-Rank
ranks.description: A barely registered hunter trusted with simple village requests.
ranks.evaluation_hint: Prove survival basics and complete a small local request.

suggested_requirements.notes: Early game. No dungeon clear required.

ranks.name: D-Rank
ranks.description: A field adventurer who can protect a village from common threats.
ranks.evaluation_hint: Complete multiple village requests and survive basic monster hunts.

suggested_requirements.notes: Should happen after the player has basic gear and one small structure clear.

ranks.name: C-Rank
ranks.description: A licensed monster hunter trusted with crypts, towers, and dangerous roads.
ranks.evaluation_hint: Clear real structures, complete contract chains, and prove combat competence.

ranks.name: B-Rank
ranks.description: An elite hunter able to clear serious dungeons and survive modded threats.
ranks.evaluation_hint: Defeat elite mobs, clear larger structures, and outpace rival hunters.

suggested_requirements.notes: Should require a meaningful boss or miniboss proof.

ranks.name: A-Rank
ranks.description: A realm-class fighter called when towns expect disaster.
ranks.evaluation_hint: Defeat major bosses, protect high-risk settlements, and handle dragon-tier zones.

suggested_requirements.notes: Late midgame. Should not happen from ordinary grind.

ranks.name: S-Rank
ranks.description: An ascendant-class anomaly whose actions become public history.
ranks.evaluation_hint: Clear realm-level threats and prove command of the full pack stack.

suggested_requirements.notes: Endgame myth tier. Should require curated achievements, not simple counters.
